sanskrit: देवद्विजगुरुप्राज्ञपूजनं शौचमार्जवम् । ब्रह्मचर्यमहिंसा च शारीरं तप उच्यते ॥
hindiTranslation: देवता, ब्राह्मण, गुरुजन और जीवन्मुक्त महापुरुषका पूजन करना, शुद्धि रखना, सरलता, ब्रह्मचर्यका पालन करना और हिंसा न करना  यह शरीरसम्बन्धी तप कहा जाता है ॥
englishTranslation: Worship of the radiant beings, the wise, the teacher, and the enlightened; purity, simplicity, celibacy, and non-violence — these are called bodily austerity.
essence: The body itself can become a disciplined offering.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: True bodily discipline includes honoring wise teachers, staying clean, living simply, practicing self-restraint, and avoiding harm.
simpleInsight: Spiritual discipline is not abstract. It begins with how you treat your body and other people.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Arjuna is frozen between duty and grief. Krishna now explains that real discipline is not only inner resolve; it also shows up in bodily conduct — respect, cleanliness, simplicity, restraint, and non-harm.
modernRelevance: You scroll past a cruel comment, then feel the urge to add one more. The verse asks for a different kind of strength: keep your body, habits, and actions clean enough to deserve your own respect.
translationLiteral: देव (radiant beings) / द्विज (twice-born) / गुरु (teacher) / प्राज्ञ (wise) / पूजनम् (worship) / शौचम् (purity) / आर्जवम् (simplicity) / ब्रह्मचर्यम् (celibacy) / अहिंसा (non-violence) / च (and) / शारीरम् (bodily) / तपः (austerity) / उच्यते (is called)
insight: It shifts discipline from private intention to visible conduct: what you do with the body reveals the quality of your practice.

quoteVariants.text: Purify the body. Honor the wise. Live without harm.
Your body becomes a practice when it stops harming.
Keep the body pure and harmless.
